channel.title: IDE on JF
channel.description: Recent content in IDE on JF

item.title: Avoid Python Package Import-Errors in VSCodium or VSCode
item.description:
If you are working with Conda environments, you might notice that VSCodium or VS Code shows “import not found” errors (red squiggly lines) even though your code runs perfectly fine in the terminal.

This happens because the IDE’s Python extension doesn’t automatically know which Conda environment to use for its background linting and IntelliSense. By explicitly setting the interpreter path, you tell the IDE exactly where to look for your installed packages.

item.title: Tune VSCodium for Python
item.description:
VSCodium is the Open-Source binaries version of the popular Visual Studio Code IDE, mainly developed by Microsoft.

However, VSCodium lacks some nice features such as code auto complete, nice code coloring, funtcion preview at howver, etc. The good news is: You can easily enhance your VSCodium Experience, by following the following steps.

1. Install Extensions

Install the

  • Python and
  • Pyright (by ms-pyright)

Extensions via the Extensions Store at the left, vertical bar in VSCodium.
